questions.question: What is the definition of per-frame encryption key?
choices: A secret text string employed to corroborate the user’s identity.
A shared, secret, and potentially low-entropy word, phrase, code, or key used as a credential for authentication purposes.

Referring to, or an attribute of, two entities that are associated with each other, e.g., an access point (AP) and an associated station (STA), or two STAs in an independent basic service set (IBSS) network.
A unique encryption key constructed for each medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU).
An aggregate multi-hop criterion used to characterize the performance, quality, and eligibility of a mesh path.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A per-frame encryption key is a unique encryption key generated for each MAC protocol data unit (MPDU).

questions.question: What is a Virtual Machine?
choices: A provider that offers virtual home environment services.
A type of telecommunication service with a fixed bit rate.
A software program that simulates a hypothetical computer central processing unit.
A provider that offers services other than basic telecommunications service for additional charges.
A type of telecommunication service with a bit rate that can vary within defined limits.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A virtual machine is a software program that simulates a hypothetical computer central processing unit.

questions.question: What does BSA stand for in the context of IEEE 802.11?
choices: Baseline Symbol Allocation
Beam Squinting Algorithm
Basic Service Area
Backward Signal Alert
Blind Search Algorithm
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: BSA stands for Basic Service Area, which refers to the area containing the members of a basic service set (BSS).

questions.question: What is the difference between SISO and MIMO systems?
choices: SISO uses a single antenna, while MIMO uses multiple antennas.
SISO has higher power efficiency than MIMO.
MIMO operates in the 2.4 GHz frequency band, while SISO uses 5 GHz.
SISO supports more users simultaneously than MIMO.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: SISO (Single Input Single Output) uses a single antenna for transmission and reception, while MIMO employs multiple antennas for improved performance.

questions.question: What is a teleaction service?
choices: A telecommunication service that uses short messages requiring a low transmission rate
A service offered by a PLMN operator or service provider to satisfy a specific telecommunication requirement
A type of telecommunication service that provides complete capability for communication between users according to standardised protocols
A type of telecommunication service that uses text conversation between users in at least two locations
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A teleaction service is a type of telecommunication service that uses short messages requiring a low transmission rate between the user and the network.

questions.question: What is the role of a mobile station (STA) in IEEE Std 802.11?
choices: Allow multiple stations to simultaneously transmit to a single STA
Support multiple BSSIDs in a single frame
Manage multiple cooperating STAs
Enable fast BSS transitions
Use network communications while in motion
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A mobile station (STA) uses network communications while in motion.

questions.question: What is the access delay?
choices: The time it takes for an access request to be processed
The time it takes for an access request to be denied
The time it takes for an access request to be granted
The time it takes for an access request to be forwarded
The time it takes for an access request to be received
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The access delay is the value of the elapsed time between an access request and a successful access.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a message integrity code (MIC)?
choices: To identify the attributes of a mesh basic service set
To enable the creation and operation of a mesh basic service set
To provide message integrity for wireless LAN communication
To manage multiple cooperating stations
To support fast BSS transitions within a mobility domain
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A message integrity code (MIC) is a value generated by a cryptographic function to provide message integrity. It helps detect changes in input data without knowledge of the cryptographic key(s) used.

questions.question: What is the definition of Central DNS resolver/server?
choices: A DNS resolver/server responsible for resolving UE DNS Queries into suitable EAS IP address(es) within the local DN.
An Application Server resident in the Edge Hosting Environment.
An environment providing support required for Edge Application Server's execution.
The set of network entities of a DN that are deployed locally.
A DNS resolver/server centrally deployed by the 5GC operator or 3rd party and is responsible for resolving the UE DNS Queries into suitable Edge Application Server (EAS) IP address(es).
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Central DNS resolver/server is a DNS resolver/server centrally deployed by the 5GC operator or 3rd party and is responsible for resolving the UE DNS Queries into suitable Edge Application Server (EAS) IP address(es).

questions.question: What is the definition of residual error rate?
choices: A parameter describing service accuracy.
The maximum gain of the receiver antenna in the horizontal plane.
The bandwidth in which an emission level is specified.
The pointer, which addresses one record in an EF.
The difference between the power of a RE and the average RE power for a BS at maximum output power.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Residual error rate is a parameter describing service accuracy, specifically the frequency of lost SDUs and corrupted or duplicated network SDUs delivered at the user-network interface.

questions.question: What is the definition of premises radio access station?
choices: A network deployment that does not interact with a public network
The uninterrupted user experience of a service during an access change
A set of network functions and corresponding resources necessary to provide telecommunication services
A base station installed at a customer premises network
A radio access network connecting to the 5G core network using NR, E-UTRA, or both
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A premises radio access station refers to a base station installed at a customer premises network.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the antenna weight vector (AWV)?
choices: To determine the size of the antenna array
To describe the excitation for each element of an antenna array
To measure the signal strength of an antenna array
To establish a mapping between an access point (AP) and a station (STA)
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The antenna weight vector (AWV) is a vector of weights that describes the excitation (amplitude and phase) for each element of an antenna array.

questions.question: What is a Hybrid Cell?
choices: A broadcast string in free text format that provides a human-readable name for the Home NodeB/eNodeB.
The factor that maintains reliability at the cell boundary.
A 3GPP access network consisting of multiple cells with different characteristics.
A cell accessible by UEs that are members of a specific group and as a normal cell by other UEs.
The process in which the radio access network changes the radio transmitters or radio access mode or radio system used to provide the bearer services.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A hybrid cell is a cell accessible as a closed cell by UEs which are members of a specific group and as a normal cell by all other UEs.

questions.question: What does the acronym MSAS stand for?
choices: Multifunctional Satellite Augmentation System
Mobile Satellite Augmentation System
Multi-Services Augmentation System
Multi-functional Satellite Augmentation System
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MSAS stands for Multi-functional Satellite Augmentation System.

questions.question: What is the definition of user experienced data rate?
choices: A service area solely relying on infrastructures and positioning technologies present anywhere 5G is present
The time taken to transfer a given piece of information from a source to a destination
The minimum data rate required to achieve a sufficient quality experience
A radio access network connecting to the 5G core network using NR, E-UTRA, or both
A set of network functions and corresponding resources necessary to provide telecommunication services
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: User experienced data rate is the minimum data rate required to achieve a sufficient quality experience.

questions.question: What is an inter PLMN handover?
choices: A handover between different cells
A handover between different PLMNs
A handover within one sector or between different sectors of the same cell
A handover between networks using different radiosystems
A handover within the same network
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: An inter PLMN handover refers to a handover between different PLMNs, regardless of the radio access system used.

questions.question: What is a Logical Model?
choices: Allows an IP-capable UE direct access to other IP-capable devices in the local network.
The frequency of the lower edge of the Base Station RF bandwidth.
A UE registers its presence in a specific area.
Abstract view of a network or network element using information objects.
Logical O&M procedures physically implemented in Node B.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A Logical Model defines an abstract view of a network or network element by means of information objects representing network element, aggregations of network elements, the topological relationship between the elements, endpoints of connections, and transport entities.

questions.question: What is the definition of Active communication?
choices: The process of apportioning charges between the Home Environment, Serving Network and User.
A UE is in active communication when it has a CS connection established.
Set of radio links simultaneously involved in a specific communication service between an UE and a UTRAN.
The ratio of the average power centered on the assigned channel frequency to the average power centered on an adjacent channel frequency.
The user rate between Mobile Termination and IWF.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Active communication refers to a UE having a CS (Circuit Switched) connection established.

questions.question: What does the acronym EGNOS stand for?
choices: European Geolocation Network Overlay System
European Geographic Navigation Overlay System
European Geo Observed Navigation System
European Geographical Network Orientation System
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: EGNOS stands for European Geographic Navigation Overlay System.

questions.question: Which networking device is used to connect multiple computers in a LAN and forward data packets between them?
choices: Router
Switch
Modem
Firewall
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A network switch is used to connect multiple devices within a LAN and forward data based on MAC addresses.

questions.question: What is quadrature binary phase shift keying (QBPSK)?
choices: A modulation technique that maps binary data onto the imaginary (Q) axis
A prioritized QoS mechanism used for updating the network allocation vector (NAV)
A function that hashes various inputs and derives a pseudorandom value
A channel of operation for all stations in a basic service set (BSS)
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Quadrature binary phase shift keying (QBPSK) is a binary phase shift keying modulation in which the binary data is mapped onto the imaginary (Q) axis.

questions.question: What is a Localised Service Area (LSA)?
choices: A specific location where logical resources are controlled by the RNC
An area in which the Core Network offers specific services
A network element that represents aggregations of network elements
The frequency reference point for transmitter and receiver requirements
The registration area where a UE registers its presence
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A Localised Service Area (LSA) is an operator-defined group of cells where specific access conditions apply and specific services are offered.

questions.question: What does Shannon's Channel Capacity represent in information theory?
choices: The maximum bandwidth of a communication channel
The minimum signal-to-noise ratio required for error-free communication
The maximum rate of reliable information transmission over a noisy channel
The total energy consumption of a communication system
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Shannon's Channel Capacity represents the highest data rate at which information can be sent through a noisy communication channel while ensuring reliable communication. It is a fundamental concept in information theory.

questions.question: What is the purpose of dynamic station (STA) enablement (DSE)?
choices: To grant permission and dictate operational procedures to enabling stations (STAs)
To interconnect basic service sets (BSSs) and integrated local area networks (LANs)
To transport MAC service tuples between stations (STAs) that are not in direct communication with each other
To detect radar signals in the 5 GHz band
To construct protected frames from unprotected frames
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The purpose of dynamic station (STA) enablement (DSE) is to grant permission and dictate operational procedures to stations that are subject to its control.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an IP address in networking?
choices: To identify the manufacturer of a network device.
To identify the geographical location of a network device.
To uniquely identify a network device on the internet or a local network.
To encrypt data transmitted over a network.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: An IP address is used to uniquely identify a network device, allowing data to be routed to the correct destination on a network.

questions.question: What is the definition of MBMS?
choices: Main Broadband Multicast Signal
Multicast-Based Multiservice
Multimedia Broadcast Multicast Service
Multiple Broadcast Multiservice
Massive Broadcast Multiservice
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MBMS stands for Multimedia Broadcast Multicast Service.

questions.question: What does NR stand for?
choices: A label used for identification of end users
Radio access technology for fifth generation
A functional group on the network side of a user-network interface
The nominal power defined by the UE power class
Protocols between UE and the core network that are not terminated in the UTRAN
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: NR stands for fifth generation radio access technology.

questions.question: What is a point-to-point service?
choices: Billing arrangement between customer and operator/service provider where the customer deposits an amount of money in advance
A service type in which data is sent from a single network termination to another network termination
A measure of throughput
A unit of data specified in an (N)-protocol layer
The geographical area in which a PLMN provides communication services
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A point-to-point service is a service type in which data is sent from a single network termination to another network termination.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Domain Specific Access Control?
choices: Obsolete term for Elementary File.
Information coded as TLV objects, i.e. consisting of a Tag, a Length and a Value part.
Access control functionality for access barring in either domain (i.e. CS domain or PS domain).
The process of deactivating the personalisation so that the ME ceases to carry out the verification checks.
A file containing access conditions and, optionally, Elementary Files (EFs) or other Dedicated Files (DFs).
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Domain Specific Access Control provides access control functionality for access barring in either the CS domain or PS domain.

questions.question: What is the meaning of IP Flow Mobility?
choices: The distribution of IP flows on a UE between 3GPP and WLAN available accesses
The flow of IP packets between two network entities
The mobility of IP addresses between different networks
The mobility of IP packets between different radio systems
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: IP Flow Mobility refers to the distribution of IP flows on a UE between 3GPP and WLAN available accesses based on the different characteristics of the IP flows, the operator policies, and the capabilities of the available accesses.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of source coding in data compression?
choices: To increase the data transmission rate
To encrypt the data for secure transmission
To reduce the size of data representation with minimal loss of information
To enhance data integrity during transmission
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Source coding, also known as data compression, aims to represent data in a more efficient form, reducing its size while preserving as much of the original information as possible.

questions.question: What is the definition of Signalling?
choices: The exchange of information between the user equipment and the core network
The transfer of higher layer information between entities in the non-access stratum
The establishment and control of connections in a telecommunications network
The transmission of user data over a radio link
The switching of calls between different networks
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Signalling refers to the exchange of information specifically concerned with the establishment and control of connections, and with management, in a telecommunications network.

questions.question: What is an E-RAB?
choices: An entity associated with one or multiple subscriptions in a network
A file containing access conditions and data on the UICC
A power saving feature where paging cycles can range from seconds to several hours
An identifier for the concatenation of an S1 Bearer and the corresponding Data Radio Bearer
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: An E-RAB (E-UTRAN Radio Access Bearer) is an identifier that uniquely identifies the concatenation of an S1 Bearer and the corresponding Data Radio Bearer. It is used in the Non Access Stratum.

questions.question: What does 'Maximum throughput' refer to?
choices: The maximum power supported by the UE
The root directory of the file system hierarchy on the UICC
The maximum RF bandwidth supported by a BS within an operating band
The maximum achievable throughput for a reference measurement channel
Cells with no radio links
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Maximum throughput refers to the maximum achievable throughput for a reference measurement channel.

questions.question: What is multi-user multiple input, multiple output (MU-MIMO) in IEEE 802.11-2020?
choices: A value generated by a cryptographic function that ensures message integrity.
A set of basic service sets (BSSs) within the same extended service set (ESS) that support fast BSS transitions.
The capability to advertise information for multiple BSSIDs using a single Beacon or Probe Response frame.
A technique by which multiple stations (STAs) either simultaneously transmit to a single STA or simultaneously receive from a single STA independent data streams over the same radio frequencies.

A framework used with admission control for the treatment of traffic streams based on precedence, which supports the preemption of an active traffic stream by a higher precedence traffic stream when resources are limited.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MU-MIMO is a technique by which multiple stations (STAs) either simultaneously transmit to a single STA or simultaneously receive from a single STA independent data streams over the same radio frequencies.

questions.question: What does IPI stand for in the context of IEEE Std 802.11-2020?
choices: Information Processing Indicator
Independent Power Indicator
Intrusion Prevention Indicator
Idle Power Indicator
Integrated Performance Indicator
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: IPI is an abbreviation for idle power indicator, which is a physical layer (PHY) indication of the total channel power (noise and interference) when a station is idle.

questions.question: What is the definition of Fallback per band feature set?
choices: A feature set per band that has higher capabilities than the reported feature set per band.
A feature set per band that has lower capabilities than the reported feature set per band.
A feature set per band that includes only non-contiguous bands.
A feature set per band that is used for sidelink carriers only.
A feature set per band that supports the same or lower capabilities than the reported feature set per band.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Fallback per band feature set has the same or lower capabilities than the reported capabilities from the reported feature set per band.

questions.question: What is the meaning of MPS-subscribed UE?
choices: A UE with a personal identification number
A UE that utilizes virtual private networks
A UE that has a subscription to a mobile network operator supporting MPS
A UE that utilizes over-the-top services
A UE with multimedia telephony capabilities
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MPS-subscribed UE refers to a UE that has a subscription to a mobile network operator that supports the MPS feature.

questions.question: What is spatial multiplexing (SM)?
choices: A network maintained by a subscription service provider
A technique for transmitting data streams on multiple spatial channels
A label for MAC service data units with distinct user priority
A set of services that support transport of MAC service data units between stations
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Spatial multiplexing (SM) is a transmission technique in which data streams are transmitted on multiple spatial channels provided through the use of multiple antennas at the transmitter and receiver.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation 'MCS' stand for?
choices: Modulation and coding scheme
Multicast broadcast services
Master cell group
Timing advance
Radio resource management
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MCS stands for Modulation and coding scheme.

questions.question: What does CA stand for?
choices: Connection Assurance
Channel Allocation
Circuit Analysis
Cellular Architecture
Carrier Aggregation
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: CA stands for Carrier Aggregation.

questions.question: What is the Home Environment?
choices: The entity responsible for overall provision and control of the Personal Service Environment of subscribers.
A category of handover procedures where all the old radio links are abandoned before establishing new radio links.
A 3GPP access network consisting of multiple cells with different characteristics.
The transfer of a user's connection from one radio channel to another.
A broadcast string in free text format that provides a human-readable name for the Home NodeB/eNodeB.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The Home Environment is responsible for the overall provision and control of the Personal Service Environment of its subscribers.

questions.question: What is the definition of additional authentication data (AAD)?
choices: The prevention of unauthorized usage of resources.
Data that are not encrypted, but are cryptographically protected.
A structure that contains one or more MSDUs and is transported within a single (unfragmented) data MAC protocol data unit (MPDU).
A structure that contains one or more MSDUs and is transported within a single (unfragmented) data medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU).
The measurement point of reference for radio frequency measurements in a station (STA).
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Additional authentication data (AAD) refers to data that are cryptographically protected but not encrypted.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation 'CM' stand for?
choices: Configuration Monitoring
Configuration Management
Content Management
Customer Management
Configuration Module
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: CM stands for Configuration Management.

questions.question: What is Synchronous Transmission?
choices: A method of data transmission where data is sent one bit at a time, asynchronously.
A method of data transmission where data is sent in a continuous stream at a constant rate, synchronized by a common clock signal.
A method of data transmission that relies on error-checking codes for data accuracy.
A method of data transmission that uses satellite links for long-distance communication.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Synchronous Transmission is a method in which data is sent in a steady, synchronized flow, with both sender and receiver synchronized by a shared clock signal for precise timing.

questions.question: What is the definition of Authentication Server (AS)?
choices: A vector of weights describing the excitation (amplitude and phase) for each element of an antenna array.
An entity that provides an authentication service to an Authenticator.
The medium access control (MAC) address of the IEEE 802.1X Authenticator’s STA.
The act of determining whether a particular right, such as access to a resource, is granted to an entity.
The horizontal orientation of the front surface of a station or of a radio antenna system’s main lobe measured clockwise from true north.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: An Authentication Server (AS) is an entity that provides an authentication service to an Authenticator. This service determines, from the credentials provided by the Supplicant, whether the Supplicant is authorized to access the services provided by the Authenticator.

questions.question: What is the definition of a Registered PLMN (RPLMN)?
choices: A particular version of the 3GPP system standards produced by the 3GPP project
A service entitlement to only certain geographical part(s) of a PLMN
The network which receives the number in the porting process
The PLMN on which the UE has performed a location registration successfully
The process of camping on a cell of the PLMN and doing any necessary LRs
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Registered PLMN (RPLMN) refers to the PLMN on which the UE has successfully performed a location registration.

questions.question: What is the definition of power control dynamic range?
choices: A formal set of procedures for communication
A measure of throughput for bursty signals
The difference between maximum and minimum transmit power
The act of seeking a User Equipment
A mode of operation to reduce power consumption
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Power control dynamic range refers to the difference between the maximum and minimum transmit output power for a specified condition.

questions.question: What does the acronym GLONASS stand for?
choices: GLObal NAvigation Satellite System
Global Location and Navigation Satellite System
Global Observation Navigation and Location Assistance System
Global Outreach Navigation and Location Access System
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: GLONASS stands for GLObal NAvigation Satellite System.

questions.question: What is a Supplicant?
choices: An entity at one end of a point-to-point LAN segment that is being authenticated by a Supplicant attached to the other end of that link.
An entity at one end of a point-to-point connection that is being authenticated by a Supplicant attached to the other end of that link.
An entity at one end of a point-to-multipoint LAN segment that is being authenticated by an Authenticator attached to the other end of that link.
An entity at one end of a point-to-multipoint connection that is being authenticated by a Supplicant attached to the other end of that link.
An entity at one end of a point-to-point LAN segment that is being authenticated by an Authenticator attached to the other end of that link.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Supplicant is an entity at one end of a point-to-point local area network (LAN) segment that is being authenticated by an Authenticator attached to the other end of that link.

questions.question: What is network subset personalisation?
choices: An association established by a network layer between two users
Allows the network operator to personalise a ME so that it can only be used with that particular network operator's (U)SIMs
A refinement of network personalisation, which allows network operators to limit the usage of a ME to a subset of (U)SIMs
A logical node responsible for radio transmission / reception in one or more cells to/from the User Equipment
Relating to general, CHV related, GSM security related and subscription related procedures
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Network subset personalisation is a refinement of network personalisation, which allows network operators to limit the usage of a ME to a subset of (U)SIMs.

questions.question: What is a space-time stream (STS)?
choices: A stream of modulation symbols created by spatial and temporal processing
A network controlled by a subscription service provider
A set of services that support transport of MAC service data units
A stream of bits transmitted over multiple spatial dimensions
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A space-time stream (STS) is a stream of modulation symbols created by applying a combination of spatial and temporal processing to one or more spatial streams of modulation symbols.

questions.question: What is the definition of MCOT?
choices: Channel access priority class
Channel Occupancy Time
Licensed Assisted Access
Max Channel Occupancy Time
Autonomoulsy Uplink\_Downlink Feedback Indication
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: MCOT stands for Maximum Channel Occupancy Time.

questions.question: What does DSL stand for in telecommunications?
choices: Dynamic Spectrum Leasing
Data System Link
Distributed Signal Line
Digital Subscriber Line
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: DSL stands for Digital Subscriber Line, a technology that provides high-speed internet access over traditional copper telephone lines.

questions.question: What is a MExE classmark?
choices: A certification for a MExE application
A category of MExE UE
An identifier for MExE servers
An executable content conforming to MExE specification
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: A MExE classmark is an identifier that represents a category of MExE UE, differentiating between the functionalities offered by different MExE UEs.

questions.question: What is the definition of ML model training?
choices: A mathematical algorithm that replicates a decision an expert would make
A function that employs an ML model and/or AI decision entity to conduct inference
The capabilities of an ML training function to adjust the parameterization of an ML model
The capabilities and associated end-to-end processes to enable an ML training function to perform ML model training
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: ML model training is defined as the capabilities and associated processes to enable an ML training function to perform ML model training.

questions.question: What is Active communication?
choices: A defined set of procedures at an interface between a user and a network
A small program that is intended not to be run on its own, but rather to be embedded inside another application
A PLMN where the UE has found a cell that satisfies certain conditions
The ratio of the average power centered on the assigned channel frequency to the average power centered on an adjacent channel frequency
A UE is in active communication when it has a CS connection established or when it has one or more Activated PDP contexts
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Active communication refers to a UE being in active communication when it has a CS connection established or when it has one or more Activated PDP contexts.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation 'AMF' stand for?
choices: Access and Mobility Management Function
Application Protocol
Access Stratum
Minimization of Drive Tests
Point to Multipoint
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: AMF stands for Access and Mobility Management Function.

questions.question: What is the Online Charging System responsible for?
choices: Collecting and processing offline charging information prior to delivery to the Billing Domain.
Performing real-time Credit-Control and transaction handling.
Introducing a vendor-independent means for the introduction of new services.
Simulating the users or control signals on the other orthogonal channels of a downlink.
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The Online Charging System is responsible for performing real-time Credit-Control and includes transaction handling, rating, online correlation, and management of subscriber accounts/balances.

questions.question: What does the term '3GPP-WLAN Interworking' refer to?
choices: Interconnectivity between 3GPP systems and WLAN networks
Interworking between 3GPP systems and WAN networks
Interworking between 3GPP and WAP protocols
Interconnectivity between 3GPP and LTE networks
Interconnectivity between 3GPP and VoIP services
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The term '3GPP-WLAN Interworking' is used to refer to interconnectivity between 3GPP systems and WLAN networks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the HSS (Home Subscriber Server)?
choices: To provide resources using a certain bearer technology
To store subscriber-related information
To manage subscriber accounts/balances
To transfer information between network elements
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: The Home Subscriber Server (HSS) is responsible for storing subscriber-related information in the telecommunications network.

questions.question: Which wireless communication technology is commonly used for short-range data exchange between devices?
choices: LTE
WiMAX
Bluetooth
Satellite Communication
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: Bluetooth is a wireless technology used for short-range data exchange between devices such as smartphones, headphones, and speakers.

questions.question: What is the function of an enabling station (STA)?
choices: To generate emergency alerts
To encapsulate plaintext data into protected frames
To control when and how a dependent station (STA) can operate
To detect radar signals in the 5 GHz band
To interconnect access points (APs) and mesh gates
questions.subject: Lexicon
questions.explaination: An enabling station (STA) has the authority to control when and how a dependent STA can operate, and communicates an enabling signal to its dependents over the wireless medium.

questions.question: What is a Dedicated File (DF)?
choices: A file used for storing personal information
A file containing access conditions and Elementary Files (EFs) or other Dedicated Files (DFs)
A file dedicated to a specific UE
A file used for delivering QoS parameters

Volt
Tesla
Watt per kilogram
Joule per meter
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The unit of specific absorption rate (SAR) is watt per kilogram.

questions.question: What does a STA need to set its receive antenna configuration to during beam refinement receive training? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: The receive antenna configuration based on previous beam refinement receive training or RXSS
A quasi-omni antenna pattern in the DMG antenna
The best known receive antenna configuration for receiving from the STA with AID equal to the value of the Other_AID subfield
The transmit antenna configuration based on previous beam refinement receive training or RXSS
A random antenna configuration
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: During beam refinement receive training, a STA needs to set its receive antenna configuration to the best known receive antenna configuration based on previous beam refinement receive training or RXSS.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Real-Time Text conversation? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: To support multimedia services with IP telephony
To support multimedia services with circuit-switched technology
To enable real-time, character by character, text conversation
To provide streaming services using packet-switched technology
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Real-Time Text (RTT) conversation is a service enabled in 3GPP networks by the Global Text Telephony ( GTT ).

questions.question: What type of data security and privacy methods does the 5G system support? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Authentication and authorization methods
Integrity protection and confidentiality methods
Protection against tampering and spoofing
End-to-end encryption
Protection of user location information
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The 5G system supports integrity protection and confidentiality methods to ensure data security and privacy.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for the 5G system to support direct device connection? [3GPP Release 19]
choices: Supporting direct device connection using different spectrum than 5GC-based communication
Supporting direct device connection for 5G LAN-type private communication
Supporting direct device connection for periodic deterministic communication
Supporting direct device connection for UEs up to 100 m distance
Supporting multicast communication between UEs using direct device connection
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The 5G system needs to support direct device connection for UEs up to 50 m distance, not 100 m.

questions.question: What is the purpose of recoverySearchSpaceId? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: To provide a configuration for PRACH transmission.
To monitor PDCCH for detection of a DCI format.
To indicate the presence of SSB-MTCAdditionalPCI.
To link a search space set for monitoring PDCCH in a CORESET.
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The recoverySearchSpaceId is used to link a search space set for monitoring PDCCH in a CORESET.

questions.question: What are the effects of RF exposure on in vitro membrane function? [IEEE C95.1]
choices: Changes in cochlear hair cells
Mechanical distortion of cochlear hair cells
Thermoelastic expansion resulting in cochlear microphonics
Changes in membrane morphology and cell surface charge distribution
Leakage of proteins through artificial and cellular membranes
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Some studies have reported changes in membrane morphology and cell surface charge distribution as a result of RF exposure.

questions.question: According to IEEE Std 802.11-2020, when can an HT STA transmit a frame with LDPC coding? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: When the Rx LDPC subfield of the HT Capabilities element contains a value of 1
When the dot11LDPCCodingOptionActivated is true
When the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT is set to HT_MF or HT_GF
When the RA of the frame corresponds to an HT STA
When the TXVECTOR parameter FEC_CODING is set to LDPC_CODING
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: An HT STA can transmit a frame with LDPC coding only if the Rx LDPC subfield of the HT Capabilities element contains a value of 1.

questions.question: What is the formula to determine the Paging Frame (PF)? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID mod W)
SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID_H mod N)
SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID_H mod TeDRX,H)
SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID mod TeDRX,H)
SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID mod N)
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The Paging Frame (PF) is determined by the equation SFN mod T= (T div N)*(UE_ID mod N), where SFN is the System Frame Number and N is a parameter determined by the DRX cycle of the UE.

questions.question: What is the duration of a frame in the downlink? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: 1 ms
2 ms
5 ms
10 ms
14 ms
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Downlink transmissions are organized into frames with 10 ms duration.

questions.question: What does the source RAT do in inter-RAT handover? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: Configures Target RAT measurement
Decides on the preparation initiation
Provides necessary information to the target RAT
Prepares radio resources in the target RAT
All of the above
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The source RAT configures Target RAT measurement, decides on the preparation initiation, and provides necessary information to the target RAT in inter-RAT handover.

questions.question: What does the 'Request-response' communication paradigm involve in management service interactions? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Streaming management data over an established connection
Requesting a subscription to receive network events
Authenticating the management service consumer
Invoking an operation and providing a response
Establishing a connection for management data streaming
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The 'Request-response' communication paradigm involves a management service producer being requested by a management service consumer to invoke an operation and providing a response based on the request.

questions.question: What is invocation in the context of the LCS client? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: An action to make the LCS feature available to a subscriber
An action taken by the service provider to remove an available LCS feature from a LCS Client’s subscription profile
An action to invoke the LCS feature
An action to bar the Location Service of a specific user
An action to remove a privacy class from a target UE’s PEL
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Invocation in the context of the LCS client is the action to invoke the LCS feature, taken by the LCS Client or automatically by the LCS server as a result of a particular condition.

questions.question: What does the NWDAF provide in the response or notification on 'QoS Sustainability'? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: QoS requirements
QoS statistics
QoS change statistics and Reporting Threshold(s)
Predictions for potential QoS changes
Both QoS statistics and predictions
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The NWDAF provides the information on the QoS change statistics and the Reporting Threshold(s) that were crossed in the response or notification on 'QoS Sustainability'.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Permanent Equipment Identifier (PEI)? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: To support user confidentiality protection
To enable roaming scenarios
To address a 3GPP subscription in different data networks
To uniquely identify the UE within the AMF
To identify the user equipment accessing the 5G System
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The PEI is defined for the UE accessing the 5G System and is used to identify the user equipment.

questions.question: Which PHY operates with characteristics that enable support of LECIM applications? [IEEE 802.15.4]
choices: TVWS-FSK PHY
LECIM FSK PHY
LECIM DSSS PHY
TVWS-OFDM PHY
TVWS-NB-OFDM PHY
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The LECIM DSSS PHY operates with characteristics that enable support of LECIM applications.

questions.question: Which interface is used for communication between the HeNB and the Core Network? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: S1 interface
S5 interface
S-GW interface
X2 interface
SGi interface
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The S1 interface is used between the HeNB and the Core Network.

questions.question: What does a UE use to determine the priority for a PUCCH transmission with multicast HARQ-ACK information? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: MCCH-RNTI
G-RNTIs
G-CS-RNTI
CRC
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: A UE determines a priority for a PUCCH transmission with multicast HARQ-ACK information according to any HARQ-ACK reporting mode using G-RNTIs for multicast.

questions.question: What does the low-frequency upturn in the U-shaped S-F curve indicate? [IEEE C95.1]
choices: The modification of the cell's resting potential due to depolarization.
The initiation of nerve excitation at a zero crossing of the sinusoidal waveform.
The slow rate of rise of the sinusoid allowing the nerve to accommodate to the stimulus.
The dependence of the induced field on the derivative of the environmental field.
The convergence of thresholds to a slope proportional to frequency for continued sinusoidal stimulation.
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The low-frequency upturn in the U-shaped S-F curve occurs because the slow rate of rise of the sinusoid allows the nerve to accommodate to the stimulus.

questions.question: According to IEEE Std 802.11-2020, what frames should a STA transmit to indicate its intent to change power management modes? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: RTS frames
Beacon frames
ATIM frames
Null frames
ACK frames
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: In order to indicate its intent to change power management modes, a STA shall transmit individually addressed or group addressed (QoS) Null frames within the ATIM window.

questions.question: What is the role of the home environment in the delegation of charging authority? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: To handle cost control mechanisms
To process charging data in real time
To control fraud in the network
To authenticate users before they incur any charges
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The role of the home environment in the delegation of charging authority is to authenticate users before they incur any charges.

questions.question: What happens when the macCapReduction is TRUE? [IEEE 802.15.4]
choices: The first superframe has the CAP and all other superframes do not have a CAP
The first superframe does not have a CAP and all other superframes have a CAP
All superframes have a CAP
None of the superframes have a CAP
The first superframe has a reduced CAP
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: When the macCapReduction is TRUE, the first superframe (superframe ID 0) in the multi-superframe has the CAP and all other superframes do not have a CAP.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the TRMS metric in LTE? [3GPP Release 14]
choices: To measure the radiated performance of a handheld UE
To verify the performance of UE antenna subsystem
To quantify the throughput levels for each UE testing condition
To measure the fading profile within certain scope and conditions
To test the sensitivity levels over the air at different UE angular rotations
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The TRMS metric in LTE is used to quantify the radiated performance of a handheld UE when receiving a rank-2 MIMO transmission.

questions.question: What is used to identify the intended receiver of a VHT NDP? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: SSID of the VHT NDP
BSSID of the VHT NDP
RA of the immediately preceding VHT NDP Announcement frame
Association ID of the VHT NDP
Group ID of the VHT NDP
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The intended receiver of a VHT NDP is identified by the RA (Receiver Address) of the immediately preceding VHT NDP Announcement frame.

questions.question: How do non-supporting UEs handle the broadcast system information in a shared network? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: They rely on the SIM/USIM card for information.
They decode the broadcast system information to determine available operators.
They request the broadcast system information from the RAN.
They ignore the broadcast system information.
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Non-supporting UEs in a shared network ignore the broadcast system information.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Authentication Algorithm Number field? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: To track progress through the authentication exchange
To announce the existence of an 802.11 network at regular intervals
To identify the type of authentication used in the authentication process
To advertise the network's capabilities
To indicate the MAC address of the access point with which mobile stations are associated
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The Authentication Algorithm Number field is used to identify the type of authentication used in the authentication process.

questions.question: When can there be two N3 tunnel terminations at the RAN for a PDU Session? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: When there are multiple PDU Session Ids for the PDU Session
When the QoS Flows of the PDU Session are split across the two RATs
When the PDU Session belongs to a specific Network Slice
When the Master RAN node is congested
When the Secondary RAN node is congested
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: If the DL User Plane traffic of some QoS Flows of a PDU Session are directed to the Secondary RAN Node while the remaining QoS Flows are directed to the Master RAN Node, there will be two N3 tunnel terminations at the RAN for such PDU Session.

questions.question: What is the SNR used for simulation of REFSENS? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: -82.8 dBm/50 MHz
0.7 dB
6 dB
-1 dB
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The SNR used for simulation of REFSENS is -1 dB.

questions.question: Which standard provides the specification for interworking between the IMS domain and the CS domain? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: RFC 3261
ITU-T Recommendation H.248
3GPP TS 23.228
ETSI TS 183 010
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The interworking between the IMS domain and the CS domain is provided as specified in 3GPP TS 23.228.

questions.question: Which access control mechanisms are supported by NG-RAN? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: RACH back off
RRC Connection Reject
RRC Connection Release
UE based access barring mechanisms
All of the above
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: NG-RAN supports access control mechanisms such as RACH back off, RRC Connection Reject, RRC Connection Release, and UE based access barring mechanisms.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the MPM procedures? [IEEE 802.15.4]
choices: To send DBS Request commands
To allocate channels to TMCTP-child PAN coordinators
To send Enhanced Beacon frames
To synchronize multiple PANs utilizing different PHY modes
To facilitate interference avoidance among multiple SUNs utilizing different PHYs
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The purpose of the MPM procedures is to facilitate interference avoidance among multiple SUNs utilizing different PHYs.

questions.question: How do schedulers assign resources between UEs in MAC in gNB? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Based on radio conditions reported by the UE
Based on the maximum transmit power of the UE
Based on the UE buffer status and QoS requirements
Based on the UE's location in the network
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Schedulers in MAC in gNB assign resources between UEs based on their buffer status and QoS requirements.

questions.question: At what core body temperature do protein denaturation and tissue damage begin? [IEEE C95.1]
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Protein denaturation and tissue damage begin at core body temperatures of 42 °C and above.

questions.question: What happens if no response is received after an elapsed PCF interframe space during the contention-free period? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: The access point moves to the next station on its polling list
The access point ends the contention-free period
The medium is released and becomes available for DCF-based access
The access point sends a CF-End frame to signal the end of the contention-free period
The access point transmits a Beacon frame to reserve the medium
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: If no response is received after an elapsed PCF interframe space, the access point moves to the next station on its polling list to retain control of the medium.

questions.question: What is withdrawal in the context of the Target UE? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: An action to make the privacy exception list with its privacy classes available to a Target UE
An action taken by the service provider to remove an available privacy class from a target UE’s PEL
An action to invoke the LCS feature
An action to bar the Location Service of a specific user
An action to remove an available LCS feature from a target UE’s subscription profile
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Withdrawal in the context of the Target UE is the action taken by the service provider to remove an available privacy class from a Target UE’s privacy exception list.

questions.question: What type of transmissions are defined for ProSe Direct Discovery and ProSe Direct Communication between UEs? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Uplink transmissions
Downlink transmissions
Sidelink transmissions
Point-to-point transmissions
Random access transmissions
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Sidelink transmissions are defined for ProSe Direct Discovery and ProSe Direct Communication between UEs.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Duration field in data frames? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: To indicate the number of frames buffered for a station
To set the time for which the medium is restricted
To specify the group bit for broadcast or multicast frames
To determine the setting for the Frame Control field
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The Duration field in data frames is used to carry the value of the Network Allocation Vector (NAV) and restrict access to the medium for the specified time.

questions.question: What type of relationship is observed between thresholds of pain in humans and ventricular fibrillation thresholds in animals? [IEEE C95.1]
choices: Linear relationship
Exponential relationship
Logarithmic relationship
Polynomial relationship
Inverse relationship
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Thresholds of pain in humans and ventricular fibrillation thresholds in animals vary approximately with the logarithm of body weight.

questions.question: What is the minimum number of grid points required for the TRP Measurement Grid with constant density? [3GPP Release 18]
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: For the TRP Measurement Grid with constant density, a minimum of 62 grid points is required.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the TRP Measurement Grid? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: To calculate the CDF of the EIRP/EIS distribution in 3D
To determine the total power radiated by the DUT in the TX beam peak direction
To determine the TX and RX beam peak direction
To perform TRP measurements taken on the sampling grid
To perform 3D Throughput/RSRP/EIS scans for RX beam peak direction
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The TRP Measurement Grid is used to perform TRP measurements taken on the sampling grid to determine the total power radiated by the DUT in the TX beam peak direction.

questions.question: What is the key characteristic of a roaming broker enabled Home Environment? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: It has a direct commercial agreement with different serving networks.
It interworks with one or more serving network operators.
It uses fraud and cost control mechanisms to ensure charges do not exceed credit limits.
It routes registration requests to its serving networks.
It is capable of operating as a roaming broker.
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The key characteristic of a roaming broker enabled Home Environment is that it is capable of operating as a roaming broker.

questions.question: What is a requirement for clock synchronisation of the 5G system? [3GPP Release 19]
choices: Support for IEEE 1588v2
Support for 3rd-party applications
Synchronize UEs with a working clock
Support for multiple sync domains
Interface for IEEE 802.1AS sync domains
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The 5G system shall provide a media-dependent interface for one or multiple IEEE 802.1AS sync domains.

questions.question: Is a subscription profile necessary for certain authorized LCS Clients internal to the PLMN? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Yes
No
Depends on the type of service
Depends on the local regulatory requirements
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: For certain authorized LCS Clients internal to the PLMN, a subscription profile may be unnecessary.

questions.question: When does a UE initiate reselection and registration on an alternative available PLMN? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: At switch-on or recovery from lack of coverage
After a successful registration on a PLMN
When a permanent PLMN restriction is detected
When a partial or temporary PLMN restriction is detected
After periodic network selection attempts
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: A UE can initiate reselection and registration on an alternative available PLMN at switch-on or recovery from lack of coverage.

questions.question: Why are one-time pads not commonly used? [IEEE 802.11]
choices: They require a short secret key
They can be reused
They are not mathematically proven
They require a perfectly random keystream
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: One-time pads are not commonly used because the keystream must be perfectly random and the same length as the data.

questions.question: Which transmission mode does the RLC sublayer use for dedicated traffic channels (DTCH)? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: Transparent Mode (TM)
Unacknowledged Mode (UM)
Acknowledged Mode (AM)
All of the above
questions.subject: Standards specifications

questions.question: What is the purpose of the UE Capability Retrieval framework? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: To report the UE's radio access capabilities to the network
To request specific capabilities from the network
To optimize the UE's power consumption
To enable sidelink communication
All of the above

questions.question: What is the adjacent channel rejection requirement for a compliant TASK PHY receiver? [IEEE 802.15.4]
choices: 0 dB
10 dB
20 dB
30 dB
40 dB

questions.question: When is the New Mode FEC field overridden in a mode switch PPDU? [IEEE 802.15.4]
choices: When the New Mode FEC field has a value of zero
When the New Mode FEC field has a value of one
When the new mode packet has an SFD
When the new mode packet does not have FEC
When the Mode Switch field is set to zero

questions.question: Which sublayer is responsible for transfer of upper layer PDUs? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: MAC sublayer
RLC sublayer
PDCP sublayer
SDAP sublayer
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: The RLC sublayer is responsible for transfer of upper layer PDUs.

questions.question: Can multiple MDA instances be deployed? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Yes, but only for cross-domain MDA
No, MDA is limited to a single instance
Yes, for both cross-domain and domain-specific MDA
Yes, but only for domain-specific MDA
Yes, but only for CN domain MDA
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Multiple MDA instances can be deployed according to deployment needs.

questions.question: How many energy saving states can cells, network elements, and network functions be in? [3GPP Release 18]
choices: None of the above
questions.subject: Standards specifications
questions.explaination: Cells, network elements, and network functions can be in either the notEnergySaving state or the energySaving state in terms of energy saving.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the HR-SBO PDU Session in roaming? [3GPP Release 18]

To enable local traffic routing in the visited PLMN
To provide access to the data network in the home PLMN
To support session breakout in V-PLMN based on the subscription

questions.question: What are the two types of CLI measurements? [3GPP Release 18]

RSRP measurement and RSSI measurement
SRS-RSRP measurement and CLI-RSSI measurement
ULI measurement and CSI measurement

questions.question: What are the two main groups of logical channels in the MAC sublayer? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: Broadcast channels and Dedicated channels
Control channels and Traffic channels
Physical channels and Transport channels
Scheduling channels and Error correction channels
Primary channels and Secondary channels

questions.question: Which 3GPP specification specifies the architecture for enabling Edge Applications in 5GS? [3GPP Release 18]

questions.question: What is the purpose of the NotificationMessageSidelink message? [3GPP Release 17]
choices: To request UE sidelink capabilities
To include the system information transmitted by a UE via SL-BCH
To indicate the failure of a PC5 RRC AS reconfiguration
To include sidelink DRX assistance information used to determine the sidelink DRX configuration
To send notification message from U2N Relay UE to the connected U2N Remote UE
